metadata.description: Workload short name, used in naming. Lower-case, 3-8 chars.
Environment label (dev|test|prod).
Primary location for all resources.
Override location for AI Search (capacity in eastus2 is constrained; westus2 often has headroom).
Tags applied to every resource.
Networking mode. standalone = no FW, no hub (create our own DNS). hub-connected = peer to BYO hub (reference existing DNS). standalone-with-firewall is a Stage B follow-up.
Component toggles. Each gates its subnet creation; compute deploy follows in Stage B.
VNet address space CIDR. Default /20 fits all 9 subnets with growth room.
Hub VNet resource ID — REQUIRED when networkMode=hub-connected. Format: /subscriptions/<sub>/resourceGroups/<rg>/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/<name>
Hub firewall private IP (next-hop for forced-tunnel UDR). When empty, no UDR is created.
Enable forced tunneling (0/0 → hub FW). Only honored when hub-connected AND hubFirewallPrivateIp is set. Set false to bring up hub-connected mode without forced tunneling (initial bring-up).
Also create the reverse hub→spoke peer. Requires write perms on hub RG. Only works for same-subscription hubs in this revision.
When networkMode=hub-connected, map of zoneFriendlyName→resourceId for existing private DNS zones living in the hub. See modules/networking/private-dns.bicep zoneCatalog for friendly keys.

When true, APIM AI Gateway becomes the single chokepoint for all Foundry / Search / KV data-plane traffic. Flips Foundry+Search publicNetworkAccess to Disabled, enables NSG enforcement on the PrivateEndpointSubnet, and allows only APIMSubnet (and explicitly-opted exceptions) to reach the PEs. Requires apim.deploy=true AND apim.networkMode in {external,internal}. Defaults to false so smoke / POC dry-runs work without VNet line-of-sight.
When chokepoint is on, also allow ContainerAppEnvironmentSubnet → PE inbound (lets CAE-hosted agent runtimes call Foundry directly, bypassing APIM). Off by default — keep APIM as the only path.

When chokepoint is on AND Foundry agent injection is enabled, allow AIFoundrySubnet → PE inbound. Required for the Standard Agent Service to reach Foundry/Search PEs. On by default; safe to leave on even without agent injection (rule has no effect when subnet is empty).
Models to deploy on the Foundry account.
Foundry projects to create as child resources.

Stage B P2: Per-project BYOR connections (flat list). Each entry: { projectName, name, category, target, authType?=AAD, metadata?, isSharedToAll?=true }. Categories: CognitiveSearch | AzureBlob | AzureKeyVault | CosmosDb | Custom.

Stage B P2: When true, register the AIFoundrySubnet on the Foundry account as the Standard Agent Service network injection point. Independent of components.containerAppsEnv.deploy — Foundry creates its own managed CAE inside the subnet for agents.
Stage B P2: When true, also create an account-level capability host (kind=Agents). Required for the Standard Agent Service to host agents. Implies enableFoundryAgentInjection should also be true.

Stage B P3: When true AND components.standaloneSearch.deploy=true AND a BYOR connection of category=CognitiveSearch has an empty target, auto-fill the target to the standalone Search service endpoint. Saves operator from copy-pasting the search endpoint.
Deploy the Phase B.4 Logic App notification stub. Ships in state=Disabled regardless.
When deployNotifications=true, flip the Logic App workflow state to Enabled.
Incoming Webhook URL for a Teams channel. SecureString — never written to logs.
Optional comma-separated fallback email list for the notifications workflow.
Optional secondary OTLP endpoint (gRPC) for the OTel collector.
Windows admin password for the jumpbox VM. REQUIRED when components.jumpvm.deploy=true. Pass via Key Vault getSecret().
SSH public key (OpenSSH single-line format) for the build-agent VM. REQUIRED when components.buildvm.deploy=true.
Internal-only Container Apps Environment. Recommended for hub-connected mode so ingress stays inside the VNet.
APIM publisher contact local-part (left of @).
APIM publisher contact domain (right of @). Not validated for ownership.
APIM publisher display name.
Enable Azure AI Content Safety category scoring (Hate/Sexual/Violence/SelfHarm) on the APIM AI API. Adds llm-content-safety policy element.
Enable Prompt Shields jailbreak detection on the APIM AI API. Combined with enableContentSafety into the same llm-content-safety element.
Content Safety severity threshold (FourSeverityLevels: 0/2/4/6). 4 = block medium+ severity.
Enable APIM semantic cache. Provisions Redis Enterprise (RediSearch) + APIM external cache + embeddings backend.
Embedding deployment name used by the semantic cache lookup policy. Must be deployed on the Foundry account.
Per-product TPM cap for the APIM `foundry-default` product. Templated into product-token-limit.xml.
Master switch for the post-deploy RBAC module. When false (default), no role assignments are emitted regardless of the object IDs below.
Entra group object ID -> Foundry Owner on the Foundry account. Highly privileged. Leave empty to skip.
Entra group object ID -> Foundry Project Manager on the Foundry account (team leads who publish agents and create projects). Leave empty to skip.
Entra group object ID -> Foundry User on the Foundry account (developers who build agents and call models). Leave empty to skip.
Entra group object ID -> Reader on the Foundry account AND on the platform RG (auditors / SREs). Leave empty to skip.
Service principal object ID -> Contributor on the platform RG (CI/CD pipelines that deploy workloads). Leave empty to skip.

variables.$fxv#1:
{
  "version": "Notebook/1.0",
  "items": [
    {
      "type": 1,
      "content": {
        "json": "## Foundry — FinOps Showback

MTD cost per subscription / project, derived from APIM AI Gateway token telemetry (`customMetrics`, namespace `AiGateway`) joined against an inline price book and per-project monthly budgets.

**Data sources**
- `customMetrics` (App Insights classic table) — emitted by the orchestrator after every chat completion. Dimensions: `MetricNamespace`, `ProjectName`, `CostCenter`, `SubscriptionId`, `ModelName`.
- Inline price book (USD per 1K tokens) — refresh via `scripts/seed-pricing-table.ps1` when ready to swap to `PRICING_CL`.
- Inline quotas (monthly USD cap per project) — refresh via custom-log seeding when ready to swap to `SUBSCRIPTION_QUOTA_CL`."
      }
    },
    {
      "type": 3,
      "content": {
        "version": "KqlItem/1.0",
        "query": "let prices = datatable(Model:string, InputPricePer1KTokens:real, OutputPricePer1KTokens:real)
[
  'gpt-4o',                  0.005,   0.015,
  'gpt-4o-mini',             0.00015, 0.0006,
  'gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18',  0.00015, 0.0006,
  'text-embedding-3-large',  0.00013, 0,
  'o3-mini',                 0.0011,  0.0044
];
let quotas = datatable(SubscriptionId:string, ProjectName:string, CostCenter:string, MonthlyQuotaUsd:real)
[
  'sub-control-001', 'foundry-controlplane', 'CC-1001',  50,
  'sub-finops-002',  'finops-platform',      'CC-2002', 100,
  'sub-demo-003',    'demo-project-c',       'CC-3003',  25
];
customMetrics
| where timestamp >= startofmonth(now())
| where tostring(customDimensions['MetricNamespace']) == 'AiGateway'
| where name in ('PromptTokens', 'CompletionTokens')
| extend ProjectName = tostring(customDimensions['ProjectName']),
         SubId       = tostring(customDimensions['SubscriptionId']),
         CostCenter  = tostring(customDimensions['CostCenter']),
         ModelName   = tostring(customDimensions['ModelName'])
| summarize Tokens = sum(valueSum) by ProjectName, SubId, CostCenter, ModelName, MetricName=name
| evaluate pivot(MetricName, sum(Tokens))
| extend PromptTokens = coalesce(toreal(PromptTokens), 0.0),
         CompletionTokens = coalesce(toreal(CompletionTokens), 0.0)
| join kind=leftouter prices on $left.ModelName == $right.Model
| extend Cost = round((PromptTokens/1000.0)*coalesce(InputPricePer1KTokens,0.0) + (CompletionTokens/1000.0)*coalesce(OutputPricePer1KTokens,0.0), 4)
| summarize MTDCost = round(sum(Cost), 4), Calls = count() by SubId, ProjectName, CostCenter
| join kind=leftouter quotas on $left.SubId == $right.SubscriptionId, $left.ProjectName == $right.ProjectName
| extend PctOfQuota = iif(coalesce(MonthlyQuotaUsd, 0.0) > 0, round(MTDCost/MonthlyQuotaUsd*100, 1), 0.0)
| project Subscription=SubId, Project=ProjectName, CostCenter, Calls, MTDCost, MonthlyQuotaUsd=coalesce(MonthlyQuotaUsd, 0.0), PctOfQuota
| order by PctOfQuota desc",
        "size": 0,
        "title": "MTD cost vs quota — by subscription & project",
        "visualization": "table",
        "gridSettings": {
          "formatters": [
            { "columnMatch": "PctOfQuota", "formatter": 8, "formatOptions": { "palette": "yellowOrangeRed" } }
          ]
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "type": 3,
      "content": {
        "version": "KqlItem/1.0",
        "query": "let prices = datatable(Model:string, InputPricePer1KTokens:real, OutputPricePer1KTokens:real)
[
  'gpt-4o',                  0.005,   0.015,
  'gpt-4o-mini',             0.00015, 0.0006,
  'gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18',  0.00015, 0.0006,
  'text-embedding-3-large',  0.00013, 0,
  'o3-mini',                 0.0011,  0.0044
];
customMetrics
| where timestamp > ago(30d)
| where tostring(customDimensions['MetricNamespace']) == 'AiGateway'
| where name in ('PromptTokens', 'CompletionTokens')
| extend ModelName = tostring(customDimensions['ModelName']), MetricName=name
| summarize Tokens = sum(valueSum) by bin(timestamp, 1d), ModelName, MetricName
| evaluate pivot(MetricName, sum(Tokens))
| extend PromptTokens = coalesce(toreal(PromptTokens), 0.0),
         CompletionTokens = coalesce(toreal(CompletionTokens), 0.0)
| join kind=leftouter prices on $left.ModelName == $right.Model
| extend Cost = round((PromptTokens/1000.0)*coalesce(InputPricePer1KTokens,0.0) + (CompletionTokens/1000.0)*coalesce(OutputPricePer1KTokens,0.0), 4)
| project timestamp, ModelName, Cost
| render columnchart kind=stacked",
        "size": 0,
        "title": "Daily spend by model (30d)"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": 3,
      "content": {
        "version": "KqlItem/1.0",
        "query": "customMetrics
| where timestamp > ago(24h)
| where tostring(customDimensions['MetricNamespace']) == 'AiGateway'
| where name == 'TotalTokens'
| extend Project = tostring(customDimensions['ProjectName']), ModelName = tostring(customDimensions['ModelName'])
| summarize Tokens=sum(valueSum), Calls=sum(valueCount) by Project, ModelName
| order by Tokens desc",
        "size": 0,
        "title": "Token usage (last 24h) by project + model"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": 3,
      "content": {
        "version": "KqlItem/1.0",
        "query": "datatable(Model:string, Region:string, Currency:string, InputPricePer1KTokens:real, OutputPricePer1KTokens:real)
[
  'gpt-4o',                  'eastus2', 'USD', 0.005,   0.015,
  'gpt-4o-mini',             'eastus2', 'USD', 0.00015, 0.0006,
  'gpt-4o-mini-2024-07-18',  'eastus2', 'USD', 0.00015, 0.0006,
  'text-embedding-3-large',  'eastus2', 'USD', 0.00013, 0,
  'o3-mini',                 'eastus2', 'USD', 0.0011,  0.0044
]
| order by Model asc",
        "size": 0,
        "title": "Current inline price book"
      }
    },
    {
      "type": 3,
      "content": {
        "version": "KqlItem/1.0",
        "query": "datatable(SubscriptionId:string, ProjectName:string, CostCenter:string, MonthlyQuotaUsd:real, AlertThresholdPct:int)
[
  'sub-control-001', 'foundry-controlplane', 'CC-1001',  50, 80,
  'sub-finops-002',  'finops-platform',      'CC-2002', 100, 80,
  'sub-demo-003',    'demo-project-c',       'CC-3003',  25, 80
]",
        "size": 0,
        "title": "Configured monthly quotas (inline)"
      }
    }
  ],
  "isLocked": false,
  "fallbackResourceIds": []
}

metadata.description: Optional Action Group resource ID. If empty, alert fires without notify (still visible in portal).
Deploy the cost-vs-quota alert. False in smoke mode (ApiManagementGatewayLlmLog table is APIM-only and KQL validator rejects missing tables).

allOf.query:
AppDependencies
| where Name startswith "tool.fabric"
| summarize p95 = percentile(DurationMs, 95) by bin(TimeGenerated, 5m)
| where p95 > 3000

let monthStart = startofmonth(now());
let prices = PRICING_CL
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by Model, Region;
let quotas = SUBSCRIPTION_QUOTA_CL
| summarize arg_max(TimeGenerated, *) by SubscriptionId, ProjectName;
union isfuzzy=true ApiManagementGatewayLlmLog
| where TimeGenerated >= monthStart
| extend Body = parse_json(tostring(column_ifexists("BackendResponseBody", "")))
| extend ProjectName = tostring(Body["project"]), SubscriptionId = tostring(Body["subscription_id"])
| extend ModelName = tostring(column_ifexists("ModelName", ""))
| extend PromptTokens = tolong(column_ifexists("PromptTokens", 0))
| extend CompletionTokens = tolong(column_ifexists("CompletionTokens", 0))
| join kind=leftouter prices on $left.ModelName == $right.Model
| extend cost = (PromptTokens/1000.0)*InputPricePer1KTokens + (CompletionTokens/1000.0)*OutputPricePer1KTokens
| summarize MTDCost = sum(cost) by SubscriptionId, ProjectName
| join kind=leftouter quotas on SubscriptionId, ProjectName
| extend PctOfQuota = (MTDCost / MonthlyQuotaUsd) * 100
| where PctOfQuota >= 80

Tags.
Log Analytics workspace for NSG/VNet diagnostics.
Component toggles. Each toggle gates whether its corresponding subnet is created.

When true, lock down the PrivateEndpointSubnet so only APIM (and explicitly-allowed exceptions) can reach Foundry/Search/KV private endpoints. Requires apim.deploy=true AND apim.networkMode in {external,internal}. Flips PE subnet privateEndpointNetworkPolicies to NetworkSecurityGroupEnabled so NSG rules actually filter PE traffic. Defaults to false to preserve dev-friendly behavior.

When chokepoint is on, also allow ContainerAppEnvironmentSubnet to reach private endpoints. Useful when CAE-hosted agent runtimes call Foundry directly (instead of going through APIM). Defaults to false — APIM-only.

When chokepoint is on AND Foundry agent injection is enabled, allow AIFoundrySubnet -> PE inbound. Required for agents to call Foundry/Search/KV. Defaults to true; set false only if agent injection is off.

variables.$fxv#0:
<!--
  inbound-emit-metrics.xml — Service-wide AI Gateway inbound policy (global scope)

  Emits per-request token + cost metrics into Application Insights with the
  dimensions you need for chargeback: SubscriptionId, Project, UseCase, CostCenter,
  ApiName. Pair with `product-token-limit.xml` at product scope.

  IMPORTANT notes for APIM policy authoring:
  1. APIM evaluates expressions inside @(...) as C#. Single quotes are CHAR
     literals — multi-char strings MUST use double quotes. We wrap the XML
     attribute in single quotes so the inner C# can use double quotes
     without entity-escaping.
  2. `<base/>` is NOT allowed at GLOBAL (service) scope — global policy IS
     the root, there's no parent to chain. `<base/>` only appears at
     product / API / operation scope.

  References (patterns inherited, code re-authored):
  - https://github.com/Azure-Samples/AI-Gateway/tree/main/labs/finops-framework
-->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <!-- Project / UseCase / CostCenter headers are required on every call.
         Accept BOTH `x-project` and `x-project-name` for client-team flexibility. -->
    <set-variable name='ProjectName'  value='@(context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("x-project", context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("x-project-name","unknown")))' />
    <set-variable name='UseCase'      value='@(context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("x-use-case","unknown"))' />
    <set-variable name='CostCenter'   value='@(context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("x-cost-center","unknown"))' />
    <set-variable name='ApimSubId'    value='@(context.Subscription?.Id ?? "anonymous")' />

    <!-- AI Gateway built-in: emit token metrics with custom dimensions.
         Per APIM schema this element is ONLY allowed in <inbound>. It hooks
         the response post-processing internally. -->
    <azure-openai-emit-token-metric namespace="AiGateway">
      <dimension name="ApiName" value="@(context.Api.Name)" />
      <dimension name="OperationId" value="@(context.Operation.Id)" />
      <dimension name="SubscriptionId" value='@((string)context.Variables["ApimSubId"])' />
      <dimension name="ProjectName" value='@((string)context.Variables["ProjectName"])' />
      <dimension name="UseCase" value='@((string)context.Variables["UseCase"])' />
      <dimension name="CostCenter" value='@((string)context.Variables["CostCenter"])' />
      <dimension name="ClientIP" value="@(context.Request.IpAddress)" />
    </azure-openai-emit-token-metric>

    <!-- Propagate W3C traceparent to downstream Foundry for OTel correlation -->
    <set-header name="traceparent" exists-action="skip">
      <value>@($"00-{Guid.NewGuid():N}-{Guid.NewGuid().ToString("N").Substring(0,16)}-01")</value>
    </set-header>
  </inbound>

  <!--
    CRITICAL: empty <backend /> at GLOBAL scope means NO forwarding — APIM returns 200 OK with
    an empty response body, silently dropping the call. The global backend section MUST contain
    <forward-request /> explicitly (or omit the <backend> element entirely). At product/API/operation
    scope use <backend><base /></backend> instead so the chain resolves to this global rule.
    Validated live 2026-06-05 against APIM StandardV2 + Foundry — empty <backend/> = 200/0-byte body.
  -->
  <backend>
    <forward-request />
  </backend>

  <outbound>
    <set-header name="x-project" exists-action="override">
      <value>@((string)context.Variables["ProjectName"])</value>
    </set-header>
    <set-header name="x-cost-center" exists-action="override">
      <value>@((string)context.Variables["CostCenter"])</value>
    </set-header>
  </outbound>

  <on-error />
</policies>
variables.$fxv#1:
<!--
  llm-content-safety.inbound.xml — INBOUND fragment

  Uses APIM's built-in <llm-content-safety> policy (preview API: 2024-09-01)
  to scan the user prompt + chat messages with Azure AI Content Safety.

  Assembled into the API policy when enableContentSafety OR enablePromptShields
  is true. Toggles:
    - shield-prompt="true"  -> Prompt Shields (jailbreak / indirect-injection detection)
    - <categories/>          -> Hate/Sexual/Violence/SelfHarm category scoring

  Backend: content-safety-backend (created in the apim module).
  AuthN:   APIM MI -> "Cognitive Services User" role on the Content Safety
           resource (or on Foundry account when Foundry's unified endpoint is used).

  Threshold semantics (FourSeverityLevels): 0 = safe, 2 = low, 4 = medium, 6 = high.
  __SAFETY_THRESHOLD__ default 4 blocks medium+ severity. Lower to 2 for strict mode.

  NOTE: snippet only — does NOT contain <policies>/<inbound>/<base/>. The
  assembler in apim-ai-api.bicep / modules/apim/main.tf injects this between
  <base/> and <set-backend-service .../>.
-->
<llm-content-safety backend-id="content-safety-backend" shield-prompt="__SHIELD_PROMPT__">
    <categories output-type="FourSeverityLevels">
        <category name="Hate" threshold="__SAFETY_THRESHOLD__" />
        <category name="Sexual" threshold="__SAFETY_THRESHOLD__" />
        <category name="SelfHarm" threshold="__SAFETY_THRESHOLD__" />
        <category name="Violence" threshold="__SAFETY_THRESHOLD__" />
    </categories>
</llm-content-safety>
variables.$fxv#2:
<!--
  semantic-cache.inbound.xml — INBOUND fragment

  APIM AI Gateway semantic cache LOOKUP. Pairs with semantic-cache.outbound.xml.

  Requires:
    - APIM external cache configured (Azure Managed Redis Enterprise with RediSearch).
      Wired via Microsoft.ApiManagement/service/caches in the apim module.
    - APIM backend `embeddings-backend` pointing at the Foundry account
      (the cache constructs /openai/deployments/{name}/embeddings under it).
    - APIM named value `azure-openai-embeddings-deployment-name` (e.g.,
      text-embedding-3-large) — selects which deployment serves the cache's
      similarity-embedding generation.

  Vary-by includes project + use-case so two teams asking the same question
  don't poison each other's cache namespace. Score 0.85 = moderately strict.

  NOTE: snippet only — no <policies>/<inbound>/<base/>.
-->
<azure-openai-semantic-cache-lookup
    score-threshold="0.85"
    embeddings-backend-id="embeddings-backend"
    embeddings-backend-auth="system-assigned">
    <vary-by>@((string)context.Variables.GetValueOrDefault("ProjectName","unknown"))</vary-by>
    <vary-by>@((string)context.Request.Headers.GetValueOrDefault("x-use-case","unknown"))</vary-by>
</azure-openai-semantic-cache-lookup>
variables.$fxv#3:
<!--
  semantic-cache.outbound.xml — OUTBOUND fragment

  Stores the model response in the semantic cache for future lookup hits.
  Pairs with semantic-cache.inbound.xml. Cache TTL = 1h.

  NOTE: snippet only — no <policies>/<outbound>/<base/>.
-->
<azure-openai-semantic-cache-store duration="3600" />
variables.$fxv#4:
<!--
  api-policy.xml.tpl — assembled API-level policy template

  Three placeholders get substituted at deploy time by the apim-ai-api module
  (one per Stage B toggle). When the toggle is off, the corresponding fragment
  resolves to an empty string and the placeholder vanishes from the policy.

  Always-on elements:
    - set-backend-service backend-id="foundry-openai"
    - authentication-managed-identity (Foundry MI auth)

  Ordering matters: content-safety FIRST (cheapest to fail-fast),
  then semantic-cache lookup (may short-circuit the backend call),
  then backend routing + MI auth.
-->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <base />
    __CONTENT_SAFETY_INBOUND__
    __SEMANTIC_CACHE_INBOUND__
    <set-backend-service backend-id="foundry-openai" />
    <authentication-managed-identity resource="https://cognitiveservices.azure.com" />
  </inbound>
  <backend><base /></backend>
  <outbound>
    __SEMANTIC_CACHE_OUTBOUND__
    <base />
  </outbound>
  <on-error><base /></on-error>
</policies>
variables.$fxv#5:
<!--
  product-token-limit.xml — Product-scope policy

  Enforces a TPM ceiling per APIM subscription per product, with HTTP 429
  on excess. Use one product per project / per cost-center for clean attribution.

  IMPORTANT: APIM evaluates @(...) as C#. Single quotes are CHAR literals.
  Multi-char strings inside @(...) MUST be double-quoted. We wrap XML
  attributes in single quotes so the C# can use double quotes natively.

  NOTE: `remaining-tokens-variable-name`, `remaining-tokens-header-name`,
  and `tokens-consumed-header-name` are NOT valid at product scope per
  APIM policy schema — they only work at API/operation scope. Soft-warning
  via x-remaining-tokens lives in the API-level policy instead.

  Tune `tokens-per-minute` per product when assigning. Default 50_000 TPM via
  __TOKENS_PER_MINUTE__ placeholder, swapped at deploy time by Bicep/TF.
-->
<policies>
  <inbound>
    <base />

    <!-- Hard cap to prevent runaway agents -->
    <azure-openai-token-limit
      counter-key="@(context.Subscription?.Id ?? context.Request.IpAddress)"
      tokens-per-minute="__TOKENS_PER_MINUTE__"
      estimate-prompt-tokens="false" />
  </inbound>
  <backend><base /></backend>
  <outbound><base /></outbound>
  <on-error><base /></on-error>
</policies>
